bugs.error_message: TypeScript: superRefinement tried to index a typed object by string — TS7053 'Element implicitly has an any type'
bugs.root_cause: The thumbnailRefinement function used `data[k]` with a string `k`, but `data` had no index signature. The compiler couldn't narrow the access.
bugs.fix: Replaced `data[k]` / `present.includes(k)` style with explicit per-key checks (if data.thumbnailContentType !== undefined → push('thumbnailContentType') …). No string indexing, no `any`.

bugs.error_message: TypeScript: StubVideo type missing s3KeyThumbnail + thumbnailContentType after Video model gained the columns
bugs.root_cause: Adding nullable columns to the Video Prisma model widened the toVideoDto input type, which the StubVideo type alias used to declare mock prisma rows. The alias needed matching optional/nullable fields.
bugs.fix: Added `s3KeyThumbnail: string | null` + `thumbnailContentType: string | null` to StubVideo and stubCreatedVideo (both null — the existing tests don't exercise the thumbnail path).

bugs.error_message: `/dashboard/published` privacy segmented control (Public/Unlisted/Private) and date-range select appeared to do nothing — list always returned the same results.
bugs.root_cause: The `listPublishedVideos()` method built a `URLSearchParams` from `params.q / .page / .pageSize` only — `params.privacy` and `params.since` were silently dropped. The server schema (`listPublishedVideosQuerySchema`) and the types (`ListPublishedVideosParams`) supported both; the hook (`useListPublishedVideos`) forwarded them; the UI (`PrivacySegmented` + date Select) emitted them. Only the wire-layer query-string assembly was missing, so the server always saw an empty filter.
bugs.fix: Added `if (params?.privacy) search.set('privacy', params.privacy);` and `if (params?.since) search.set('since', params.since);` next to the existing `q`/`page`/`pageSize` lines in `listPublishedVideos()`. The server schema accepts 'all' and transforms it to undefined, so passing the raw value is fine.

bugs.error_message: In-memory cache limited to single-process; wanted Redis without touching the 4 service call sites + 1 test mock.
bugs.root_cause: cache singleton was eagerly instantiated as `new InMemoryCache()` at module load with no env-awareness. No way to inject Redis at boot.
bugs.fix: Refactored cache.ts: kept CacheClient interface + InMemoryCache class; added RedisCacheClient (ioredis lazyConnect + PING); replaced eager `cache` singleton with a delegating object whose methods forward to whichever backend initCache(env) picked. Added initCache(env) / verifyCache(env) / async disposeCache(). Boot-time service checks (Database + Cache + Queue) wired into apps/api/src/index.ts and apps/worker/src/index.ts before listen(), with ✓/✗ banner + hard-fail on required-service outage. Production-aware warnings in env.ts when REDIS_URL is unset.

bugs.error_message: Wrong reference: ffmpeg should be ffmpegCmd
Container clipflow-worker in restart loop: `Error [ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND]: Cannot find package 'ioredis' imported from /app/apps/worker/dist/index.js`
bugs.root_cause: pnpm uses isolated mode by default — each workspace package's direct deps live under apps/<pkg>/node_modules/<dep> as relative symlinks into .pnpm/. The Dockerfile's runtime stage copied /app/node_modules but not /app/apps/worker/node_modules, so the worker's direct `import { Redis } from "ioredis"` could not resolve (the .pnpm/ioredis blob was present, just unreachable). Latent bug — surfaced after ioredis was added to apps/worker/package.json for the Redis-cache refactor.
bugs.fix: Added `COPY --from=build --chown=nodejs:nodejs /app/apps/worker/node_modules ./apps/worker/node_modules` to the Dockerfile runtime stage, with a long comment explaining the pnpm-isolated-mode + Docker pitfall so the next person doesn't strip it.

bugs.error_message: Runtime error at boot: `headers` was called outside a request scope (next-dynamic-api-wrong-context). Stack pointed at apps/web/middleware.ts:4 — `export default NextAuth(authConfig).auth()`.
bugs.root_cause: Next.js 16 + Turbopack bundles middleware.ts with the RSC path. Calling `NextAuth(authConfig).auth()` at module-evaluation time dispatches into Auth.js v5 code that calls `next/headers`'s `headers()` — which only works inside a request scope. Eager invocation = no request scope = crash. Must export the function reference (`.auth`, no parens) and let Next.js invoke it per-request.
bugs.fix: Changed `export default NextAuth(authConfig).auth();` to `export default NextAuth(authConfig).auth as NextMiddleware;`. Cast to `NextMiddleware` from `next/server` suppresses TS2742 — the inferred type of `.auth` can't be named without referencing next-auth's private `lib/` and `lib/types` subpaths. The runtime contract is unchanged; the cast is purely structural.

bugs.error_message: ReferenceError: Cannot access 'mockX' before initialization (vitest TDZ when vi.mock factory references top-level const mocks)
bugs.root_cause: vitest hoists vi.mock() factory calls to the top of the file before module-level const declarations are initialised. The factory closure references the mocks by name and hits a TDZ error at hoist time.
bugs.fix: Wrap mock declarations in `vi.hoisted(() => ({ ... }))` so they resolve before vi.mock factories run. Standard pattern: https://vitest.dev/api/vi.html#vi-hoisted

bugs.error_message: Wrote apps/llm/llm-errors.ts by accident — file path was apps/<pkg>/src/lib/llm/ not apps/llm/. Caught on directory listing.
bugs.root_cause: Path mental model was wrong. The repo has apps/<pkg>/src/lib/<feature>/, not apps/<feature>/. The directory apps/llm/ at the workspace root doesn't exist as a convention — every feature lives inside an app.
bugs.fix: Moved apps/llm/llm-errors.ts → apps/worker/src/lib/llm/llm-errors.ts via mv, removed apps/llm/ directory. Going forward: double-check the destination directory with a quick `ls apps/<pkg>/src/lib/` before Write on a new sub-feature.

bugs.error_message: User-reported: 'the transation complete status is not showing over there.' Dashboard stayed on TRANSCRIBING badge after the worker flipped the row to GENERATING.
bugs.root_cause: SSE `STATUS_UPDATE` was the ONLY trigger for TanStack Query cache invalidation. If the SSE connection silently failed (Redis blip, dropped EventSource reconnection, channel-prefix drift between worker + API), the dashboard never refreshed and the row stayed on the old badge forever. `hasUnpublishedVideos` gating in `useVideoSSE` was also initially `videos.length > 0` — fine for the steady state, but transient SSE failures between renders had no recovery path.
bugs.fix: Added a 15 s safety-net polling refetch in DashboardContent, gated on `hasInFlightVideo` (any row in a non-final status: UPLOADED/EXTRACTING/TRANSCRIBING/GENERATING/READY/SCHEDULED/PUBLISHING/PUBLISH_FAILED/FAILED). The interval is free in the steady state — clears the moment no in-flight rows remain. Memoized the `videos` array via useMemo so the polling effect's dep array stays stable across renders.

bugs.error_message: TypeScript: 'Cannot find package zod imported from apps/worker/src/lib/llm/schemas.ts'. schemas.test.ts + validate-with-retry.test.ts both fail to load.
bugs.root_cause: Worker package never declared zod as a direct dep — it was only available transitively through @clipflow/config. When schemas.ts started importing { z } from 'zod', pnpm's isolated-mode resolution couldn't find it under apps/worker/node_modules.
bugs.fix: Added `"zod": "^3.23.8"` to apps/worker/package.json dependencies (matching @clipflow/config's version). Following the pnpm + Docker pitfall in bug-048, also note: any direct dep added to a workspace package needs to land in apps/<pkg>/node_modules at container build time — the Dockerfile's runtime COPY already covers this for worker, so no additional change there.

bugs.error_message: TypeScript: 'Argument of type undefined is not assignable to parameter of type Headers' on every OpenAI SDK error class constructor in llm-errors.test.ts.
bugs.root_cause: The openai SDK v6+ requires a `Headers` instance (not `undefined`) as the fourth constructor arg of every error subclass (AuthenticationError, NotFoundError, RateLimitError, etc.). The tests were passing `undefined` as a placeholder for 'no headers'.
bugs.fix: Added an `emptyHeaders(): Headers => new Headers()` helper at the top of llm-errors.test.ts, used as the final arg of every error construction. Tests don't care about the headers — only the status + error fields drive classifyLlmError.

bugs.error_message: TypeScript: 'Type ChaptersJson is not assignable to type NullableJsonNullValueInput | InputJsonValue. Index signature for type string is missing in type ChaptersJson.'
bugs.root_cause: Prisma's InputJsonValue requires an explicit [key: string]: unknown index signature. The ChaptersJson interface declared `summary: string` + `chapters: {...}[]` without one, so the structural-typing check fails at the prisma.video.update call site.
bugs.fix: Cast at the call site: `chaptersJson: toChaptersJson(validated.output) as unknown as object`. Prisma serializes through JSON.stringify regardless of the structural type, so the cast is purely nominal — runtime behavior is identical. Kept the cast narrow (only at the persistence boundary) so the rest of the function still uses the typed ChaptersJson shape.

bugs.error_message: TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'data'). In generate.test.ts, mapping mockPrismaVideoUpdate.mock.calls with `([, args]) => args.data?.status` returned [undefined, undefined] instead of the expected statuses.
bugs.root_cause: Off-by-one in mock.calls destructuring. vi.fn() records each call as an inner array — `prisma.video.update({ where, data })` is a single-arg call, so `mock.calls[0]` is `[{...}]`. `[, args]` skips the first element (which is the args object) and captures index 1 (always undefined for a single-arg call). The off-by-one only matters for filter/map — filter with a falsy predicate accidentally returns [] (test passes by luck), but map captures the wrong element and surfaces the bug.
bugs.fix: Changed `([, args])` → `([args])` everywhere it appears in the file. NOTE: the same off-by-one exists in transcription.test.ts:370 — the existing test passes because filter's predicate is false for undefined args.data, but it's a latent bug that should be fixed in a follow-up.

bugs.error_message: PATCH /api/videos/:id payload of { summary } alone must NOT clear existing chapters; explicit null description must clear it (not preserve it).
bugs.root_cause: Partial-merge filter was checking input.key !== undefined, which conflates absent keys with explicit null — so a null description was ignored and the old text remained. Same risk applied to chaptersJson: a save with only summary would write { summary, chapters: undefined } and clobber existing chapters.
bugs.fix: Switched to `key in input` so explicit null survives the merge (matches useUpdateProfile convention). Re-derive chaptersJson from existing.chaptersJson ?? { summary: "", chapters: [] } so absent chapters preserves prior values. Tested in videos.service.test.ts describe(updateVideo).

bugs.error_message: Dialog backdrop missing, no enter/exit animations — choppy feel
bugs.root_cause: tailwindcss-animate was listed in package.json but never registered via @plugin in globals.css. In Tailwind v4, plugins must be declared with @plugin "pkg-name" in the CSS entrypoint — the old tailwind.config.js plugins array is no longer read. Without registration, all animate-in/animate-out/fade-in-0/zoom-in-95 etc. utilities generate no CSS, so shadcn Dialog backdrop and content transitions are silent no-ops.
bugs.fix: Added @plugin "tailwindcss-animate"; as the second line of apps/web/app/globals.css, immediately after @import "tailwindcss";

bugs.error_message: Test timed out in 5000ms — all 8 publish-button.test.tsx tests hung on userEvent.click against the Sheet trigger. vi.useFakeTimers() in beforeEach collides with radix Sheet portal mounting (uses setTimeout for focus + portal placement).
bugs.root_cause: vi.useFakeTimers() in beforeEach freezes radix Dialog/Sheet portal timers, hanging userEvent. Tests appear stuck at click() and never complete.
bugs.fix: Drop vi.useFakeTimers() entirely. Use real timers and await deferred side effects via `await act(() => new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 250)))` for the 200ms redirect.

bugs.error_message: YouTube-min error test got "Schedule time must be in the future." instead of "at least 15 min" — Date constructed as Date.now() + 5 min parsed to past when sliced via .toISOString().slice(0, 16) and re-parsed by new Date("YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm") in IST timezone.
bugs.root_cause: .toISOString() returns UTC; .slice(0, 16) strips the Z marker; new Date("YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm") is then parsed as LOCAL time. In non-UTC TZ (e.g. IST = UTC+5:30), the same wall-clock string can represent a different UTC instant — often hours earlier.
bugs.fix: Build the datetime-local string from local-time getters: `${y}-${pad(M+1)}-${pad(D)}T${pad(h)}:${pad(m)}`. This round-trips through new Date() to the same ms-since-epoch regardless of TZ.

bugs.error_message: userEvent.type on datetime-local input causes per-keystroke validation against partial strings ("2026-07-02T20:5" parses as a different time than the final "2026-07-02T20:55"). Submit button flips to disabled mid-typing.
bugs.root_cause: Sheet re-runs validateScheduledAt on every onChange. userEvent.type fires one keystroke at a time, so each intermediate state triggers validation. Partial strings often parse to the past, disabling the submit button before the final value is set.
bugs.fix: Use fireEvent.change(input, { target: { value: futureLocal } }) to set the value atomically. Reserve userEvent.type for inputs without per-keystroke validation (text inputs that don't gate the submit button).

bugs.error_message: User-reported: 'worker unable to upload the chapters over the google.' Videos published successfully but no chapter markers appeared on YouTube. The enriched description was being sent with `NaN:NaN summary` style lines instead of valid `MM:SS Chapter Title` timestamps.
bugs.root_cause: `chaptersJson` on the Video row was persisted as `{ summary, chapters[] }` after the v1.5 schema change (see cerebrum 2026-07-01), but the publish path still cast it directly as `{ startMs, title }[]`. `formatChaptersForDescription` was called with the wrapper object, not the inner array. Its guard `chapters.length < 3` evaluated `undefined < 3` (false), so it proceeded to map over the object's string + array keys and emit `NaN:NaN` lines — which YouTube cannot parse as chapter timestamps. Video published cleanly, no error surfaced, chapters silently disappeared.
bugs.fix: Two-layer fix. (1) `packages/youtube-upload/src/publish-video.ts` extracts chapters via a new local `extractChapters()` helper that handles the `{ summary, chapters[] }` shape AND the pre-v1.5 bare-array shape (back-compat for any in-flight legacy rows). (2) `packages/youtube-upload/src/youtube-api.ts → formatChaptersForDescription` is now shape-tolerant — accepts the bare array OR the wrapper object, returns `""` for anything else. Three regression tests added to youtube-api.test.ts pinning the wrapper-object path so a future refactor that flattens back to the bare array can't silently reintroduce the NaN:NaN output.

bugs.root_cause: Function uses await but wasn't declared async
bugs.fix: Added async modifier | Also: // Activate the Express 4 async-error monkey-patch. Idempote; // side-effect import above installs it as soon as Express i

bugs.error_message: POST /api/videos/:id/publish hangs forever; OPTIONS 204 lands, POST sits pending until socket timeout. Server log: "Unhandled promise rejection reason: {}". Console error references videos.scheduledPublishAt, videos.youtubeVideoId, videos.createdAt, videos.updatedAt, videos.publishedAt - i.e. the Prisma update call in the publish controller rejected but the response never went out.
bugs.root_cause: Express 4 does not auto-forward async handler rejections to next(err). Every controller in this codebase is async (req, res) => { ... } with no try/catch - the same pattern as express-async-errors. When the Prisma update rejects, the returned promise becomes an unhandledRejection, the response is never sent, and the request hangs until socket timeout. pino logged "reason: {}" because Prisma errors expose message/stack via getters/non-enumerable properties the default serializer collapses to {} - masking the real cause.
bugs.fix: Added lib/async-handler.ts that monkey-patches Layer.prototype.handle_request to .catch(next) returned promises (semantically equivalent to express-async-errors, zero new deps). Wired via side-effect import in app.ts after import express and before any Router() construction (import order: import express -> import async-handler -> ...). Hardened the unhandledRejection handler in index.ts to use util.inspect (depth: 6) so future Prisma-style errors log with full detail. Updated cerebrum + memory.

bugs.error_message: Publish / unpublish requests that failed on the YouTube side surfaced a generic '{ error: INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, message: Something went wrong. Please try again. }' 500 in the frontend instead of the real reason (quota, needs-reauth, invalid metadata, channel-not-connected, etc.).
bugs.root_cause: @clipflow/youtube-upload throws its own domain error classes (`PermanentPublishError` with a `reasonCode`, `TransientPublishError`). Neither is an `AppError`, so the central error middleware (apps/api/src/middleware/error.ts) treated them as 'unknown error' → generic 500 with a canned message. The real message was logged server-side but never crossed the wire.
bugs.fix: Added a `mapYouTubeErrorToAppError` helper + `PERMANENT_REASON_TO_APP` mapping table in videos.service.ts that translates each PermanentReasonCode to an appropriate (statusCode, code) tuple and re-throws as `AppError` (preserving the upstream message and stashing `reasonCode`/`upstreamStatus` inside `details`). Wrapped `publishVideoOnYouTube` in `publishVideoNow` and `unpublishVideoOnYouTube` in `unpublishVideo` with try/catch that runs errors through the mapper. Also introduced `ApiError` in apps/web/lib/api-client.ts (mirroring `ServerApiError`) so the client-side `request()` no longer discards the `code` + `details` from the failure envelope.

bugs.root_cause: Code path had no error handling — exceptions would propagate uncaught
Function uses await but wasn't declared async

bugs.error_message: POST /api/videos/vid_14afd62d-9474-4dc3-9b0c-ca6e7416a3ba/publish hangs. Server log: "Unhandled promise rejection detail: { message: chapters.map is not a function, stack: TypeError: chapters.map is not a function at formatChaptersForDescription (...packages/youtube-upload/dist/youtube-api.js:229:28) at publishVideo (...dist/publish-video.js:66:27) at async publishVideoNow (apps/api/src/modules/videos/videos.service.ts:955:14) at async publishVideoController (apps/api/src/modules/videos/videos.controller.ts:291:18) }".
bugs.root_cause: Two-layer: (1) formatChaptersForDescription in src was shape-tolerant for bare arrays and for { summary, chapters[] }, but if `chapters` was an object/string/number/etc., it stored it into `list` and called list.map() → TypeError. (2) The earlier src-side fix from bug-125 was correct, but `dist/` had not been rebuilt — the API process imports `@clipflow/youtube-upload` via package.json main/exports which points at ./dist/index.js, so the runtime call hit the OLD unrefactored compiled output (no extractChapters, no array-unwrap), and the same TypeError fired on the { summary, chapters[] } wrapper because the wrapper is not an array.
bugs.fix: (1) Hardened formatChaptersForDescription: any non-array top-level input -> null; then Array.isArray(list) guard before list.map(); any non-array `list` returns "" instead of throwing. (2) Rebuilt dist via `pnpm --filter @clipflow/youtube-upload build` so the API process loads the fixed compiled code. (3) Added two regression tests: wrapper whose chapters field is an object/string/number/null, and bare non-array inputs.

bugs.error_message: Replicate API rejected: 'Prefer: wait=x header must specify a value between 1 and 60'
bugs.root_cause: Replicate's API caps the Prefer: wait=X header at 1-60 seconds. The `replicate` SDK translates `wait.timeout` directly into that header (lib/predictions.js:32: `Prefer: wait=${n}`), so passing `timeout: 60000` produces `Prefer: wait=60000` which violates the constraint. The SDK still polls client-side beyond the timeout, so the right value is the API ceiling (60), not the desired total wait.
bugs.fix: Changed `wait: { mode: "block", interval: 1000, timeout: 60_000 }` → `wait: { mode: "block", interval: 1000, timeout: 60 }`. Updated the comment to call out that `timeout` is what goes into the Prefer header (capped at 60), and that the SDK handles client-side polling beyond that.

bugs.root_cause: Async call without await — returned Promise instead of value

bugs.error_message: Raw upstream service error strings (Replicate 402 Payment Required, Gemini 429, AssemblyAI, FFmpeg, YouTube quota, etc.) shown verbatim to end users in VideoCard dashboard rows and published/[id]/page. Also no retry path for FAILED rows.
bugs.root_cause: VideoCard rendered `video.failureReason` directly to the user; no client-side mapper from raw upstream error strings to user-facing copy. SSE progress bar rendered alongside the FAILED status pill, confusingly showing a frozen 100% bar. No API route or UI affordance to retry a FAILED row.
bugs.fix: Added apps/web/lib/friendly-error.ts regex-based mapping (raw → {message, hint?, raw}); replaced raw text in VideoCard and published/[id]/page with friendly message + hint; hid the SSE progress bar on FAILED rows; added POST /api/videos/:id/retry (resets FAILED→EXTRACTING, re-enqueues with deterministic ingest-${videoId} jobId for BullMQ dedupe); added api.retryVideo + useRetryVideo hook + <RetryButton> client component on both the dashboard card and the detail ActionPanel.

bugs.error_message: Disconnecting YouTube account cascade-deletes all videos (DB + S3). Videos should persist — only publish/unpublish actions should be hidden.
bugs.root_cause: disconnectYouTubeChannel called prisma.youTubeChannel.deleteMany(), which triggered the Prisma onDelete: Cascade on YouTubeChannel → Video relation, deleting every associated video row.
bugs.fix: Changed to prisma.youTubeChannel.updateMany({ data: { status: "DISCONNECTED" } }) so the row persists with DISCONNECTED status. Videos remain in the DB. Existing requireConnectedChannel already checks status !== "CONNECTED", so publish/upload are already blocked. Added server-side YouTube connection fetch on video detail page to gate PublishButton and UnpublishButton behind channelConnected.

bugs.error_message: apps/web/Dockerfile was a generic single-package Next.js Dockerfile: globs package-lock.json/yarn.lock/pnpm-lock.yaml (only matches one per project), runs `npm run build` (the project uses pnpm + turbo), and COPYs `.` into the image indiscriminately. In a Turborepo layout the install layer would never find the workspace packages — the build would resolve against a broken tree and the runtime COPY of `.next/standalone` would land on an empty directory because next.config.js had no `output: "standalone"`.
bugs.root_cause: Dockerfile authored as a generic Next.js template, not the Turborepo project it actually lives in. The COPY/install/build pattern is single-package; the project's packages live outside the COPY context, the build command is `pnpm --filter web... build` not `next build` directly, and the standalone output path is `apps/web/.next/standalone/apps/web/server.js` (source path preserved) not `./server.js`.
bugs.fix: Rewrote apps/web/Dockerfile to mirror apps/worker/Dockerfile's 3-stage pnpm+frozen-lockfile pattern: deps stage COPIes workspace manifests + every package's package.json + the lockfile, then `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --filter web...`; build stage COPIes apps/web + packages, then `pnpm --filter web... build` (the trailing `...` is load-bearing — without it @clipflow/config + @clipflow/types never reach apps/web/node_modules and next build fails to resolve their imports); runtime stage COPYs `apps/web/.next/standalone` (preserving the apps/web/ source path) + static + public + apps/web/node_modules (the pnpm-isolated-mode pitfall bug-048 warns about) + the root node_modules + packages/ (workspace-package dist/ is NOT inlined by standalone). Also added `output: "standalone"` + `outputFileTracingRoot: path.join(import.meta.dirname, "../../")` to apps/web/next.config.js — both are prerequisites for the Dockerfile to function. CMD is `["node", "apps/web/server.js"]` because standalone preserves the source path inside the bundle.

bugs.error_message: User-reported: docker-compose stack had no migration step. New Prisma migrations added under packages/db/prisma/migrations/ could not be applied to the running Postgres without `docker compose down -v` (wiping local data).
bugs.root_cause: The Postgres container bootstraps from neon_backup.sql (pg_restore on first volume init) but no service in docker-compose ran `prisma migrate deploy` afterwards. Worker + api started against the dump-loaded schema, so newly-added migrations were silently skipped.
bugs.fix: Added a one-shot `migrate` service: builds from packages/db/Dockerfile.migrate (single-stage node:24.13.0-slim + corepack pnpm + @clipflow/db), CMD runs packages/db/scripts/migrate.mjs. The script probes `SELECT 1 FROM "users" LIMIT 1` to detect schema bootstrap; if present (dump-loaded), it iterates prisma/migrations/*/ and runs `prisma migrate resolve --applied <dirname>` for each to populate _prisma_migrations without re-running SQL; if absent (truly fresh), it just runs `migrate deploy` from scratch. Then `migrate deploy` picks up any genuinely-new migrations. `restart: "no"` (mirrors minio-init), `api` + `worker` both gate on `migrate: service_completed_successfully`.
